summary: pi via the REAL mcpbridge → executor → real execute_shell_command: read a build-id file, write a markdown table, read it back — streamed at the mcpagent layer
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

issues: DELTA MARKER NOT PROPAGATED TO mcpagent LAYER: pi streams token-level deltas (visible here: '...print the project build ID from the' + ' build_id.txt file.'; and the table split '...| build_id |' + ' BUILD_ID_d2954ce0b5f7 |...'). The provider-layer StreamAssistantText fix concatenates such deltas verbatim, but processChunks does NOT copy the ContentDeltaMetadataKey onto the content StreamingChunkEvent, so a mcpagent-layer consumer cannot tell pi's chunks are deltas and a naive '\n'-join would garble them. Follow-up: carry the delta marker (like Source) onto StreamingChunkEvent.
pi surfaces the bridge tool under the generic marker name 'mcp' (5x) rather than execute_shell_command.
agent_review.notes: Real-bridge path exercised end to end: pi's 'mcp' calls went mcpbridge → executor → real execute_shell_command to read build_id.txt, write report.md, and read it back. report.md on disk is a correct markdown table with BUILD_ID_d2954ce0b5f7 (only ever in the file), and pi's final reply is itself a clean GFM markdown table. The clean stream (Source != terminal) carries the interleaved narration + the table. Signed good; the two granularity items above are tracked follow-ups, not failures of the bridge path.
